zh.lifeSummary: 當William Anthony Cave於1535年出生在英國諾薩普頓史爾斯坦福 奧 阿沃時，他的父親Richard Cave29歲，母親Barbara Fielding26歲。他於1560年與Eleanor Margaret de Grey在英國萊希斯特斯爾梅爾敦 莫布雷結婚。他們至少是7個兒子和6個女兒的父母。他去世於1614年4月3日在英國英格蘭萊希斯特斯爾皮可維爾享年79歲，埋葬在英國英格蘭諾薩普頓史爾艾頓。

zh-hans.lifeSummary: 当William Anthony Cave于1535年出生在英国諾薩普頓史爾斯坦福 奧 阿沃时，他的父亲Richard Cave29岁，母亲Barbara Fielding26岁。他于1560年与Eleanor Margaret de Grey在英国萊希斯特斯爾梅爾敦 莫布雷结婚。他们至少是7个儿子和6个女儿的父母。他去世于1614年4月3日在英国英格兰萊希斯特斯爾皮可維爾享年79岁，埋葬在英国英格兰諾薩普頓史爾艾頓。

nameMeanings.name: Cave
nameMeanings.attribution: Dictionary of American Family Names © Patrick Hanks 2003, 2006.
meanings: English: habitational name from a place in East Yorkshire called Cave, apparently from a river name derived from Old English cāf ‘swift’.
English (of Norman origin): nickname from Norman and Picard Old French cauf (from Latin calvus) ‘bald’.
French: topographic name for someone who lived in or near a cave, from Old French cave ‘cave’, ‘cellar’ (from Latin cavea, a derivative of cavus ‘hollow’).

variants.name: Cavin
meanings: Scottish (Wigtownshire): variant of Caveen, a shortened form of Manx Mac Dhaimhín, son of Daimhín. The personal name is a diminutive of Gaelic damh ‘bard, poet’.

variants.name: Caves
meanings: English: variant of Cave , with post-medieval excrescent -s.

variants.name: Keeve
meanings: English: perhaps a variant of   Cave  . This surname is rare in Britain.

variants.name: Chaffee
meanings: English (of Norman origin): descriptive nickname from a derivative of Old French chauf ‘bald’ (from Latin calvus). Compare Cave .

variants.name: Cavett
meanings: Scottish (of French origin): altered form of French Cavet, which is either a diminutive of Cave 3 or a metonymic occupational name for a laborer, one who used a cavet, an Old Occitan word for a tool for clearing ground for cultivation.

nameMeanings.name: William
nameMeanings.attribution: Dictionary of First Names © Patrick Hanks and Flavia Hodges 1990, 2003, 2006.
meanings: Probably the most successful of all the Old French names of Germanic origin that were introduced to England by the Normans. It is derived from Germanic wil ‘will, desire’ + helm ‘helmet, protection’. The fact that it was borne by the Conqueror himself does not seem to have inhibited its favour with the ‘conquered’ population: in the first century after the Conquest it was the commonest male name of all, and not only among the Normans. In the later Middle Ages it was overtaken by John , but continued to run second to that name until the 20th century, when the picture became more fragmented.

nameMeanings.name: Anthony
nameMeanings.attribution: Dictionary of First Names © Patrick Hanks and Flavia Hodges 1990, 2003, 2006.
meanings: The usual English form of the old Roman family name Antonius, which is of uncertain (probably Etruscan) origin. The spelling with -th- (not normally reflected in the pronunciation) represents a learned but erroneous attempt to associate it with Greek anthos ‘flower’. In the post-classical period it was a common name, borne by various early saints, most notably a 3rd-century Egyptian hermit monk, who is regarded as the founder of Christian monasticism.

historicEvents.title: Book of Common Prayer
historicEvents.description: Book of Common Prayer, a product of the English Reformation, was published in 1549 for assistance in the administration of the sacraments and other rites and ceremonies of the church according to the use of the Church of England. The book outlined morning, evening, and communion prayers and orders for baptism and marriage, thus making England a truly Protestant state.

historicEvents.title: Act of Uniformity
historicEvents.description: The Act of Uniformity was passed by the Parliament of England and required all people to go to church once a week. The consequence of not attending church was a fine of 12 pence, which was a considerable amount for a poor person.

historicEvents.title: State Lottery
historicEvents.description: A State Lottery was recorded in 1569. The tickets were sold at St. Paul's Cathedral in London.

historicEvents.title: The First English Colony is Established in America
historicEvents.description: England established its first colony in North America in 1585 as a way to generate additional wealth. The colony was named Virginia, after the virgin Queen Elizabeth I.

historicEvents.title: Elizabeth I Signs Death Warrant for Mary, Queen of Scots
historicEvents.description: Elizabeth I signed the death warrant for Mary, Queen of Scots, on February 1, 1587, as she was found guilty of plotting to assasinate Elizabeth. Mary, Queen of Scots, was beheaded at Fotheringhay Castle in England on February 8, 1587.
historicEvents.urlTitle: Elizabeth I.org - Mary Queen of Scots

historicEvents.title: The Honourable East India Company
historicEvents.description: The Honourable East India Company, a British joint-stock company, was established in 1600 for trade in the Indian Ocean region. At its height, the British East India Company had a private army which was twice the size of the British Army, ruled large sections of India, and revenues in the millions.
historicEvents.urlTitle: Historic UK - The East India Company

en.lifeSummary: When William Anthony Cave was born in 1535, in Stanford on Avon, Northamptonshire, England, his father, Richard Cave, was 29 and his mother, Barbara Fielding, was 26. He married Eleanor Margaret de Grey in 1560, in Melton Mowbray, Leicestershire, England. They were the parents of at least 7 sons and 6 daughters. He died on 3 April 1614, in Pickwell, Leicestershire, England, United Kingdom, at the age of 79, and was buried in Etton, Northamptonshire, England, United Kingdom.

Il a épousé Eleanor Margaret de Grey en 1560, à Melton Mowbray, Leicestershire, Angleterre. Ils ont eu au moins 7 fils et 6 filles. Il est décédé le 3 avril 1614, à Pickwell, Leicestershire, Angleterre, Royaume-Uni, à l’âge de 79 ans, et a été enterré à Etton, Northamptonshire, Angleterre, Royaume-Uni.

ko.lifeSummary: William Anthony Cave(이)가 1535년 영국 노르섬프토실르 스탠퍼드  아폰에서 태어났을 때, 그의 아버지 Richard Cave(은)는 29세였고, 그의 어머니 Barbara Fielding(은)는 26세였다. 그는 1560년 영국 레이스스터실르 멜턴 모우브라이에서 Eleanor Margaret de Grey(와)과 결혼했다. 그들은 적어도 일곱 명의 아들과 여섯 명의 딸을 두었다. 그는 1614년 4월 3일 영국 잉글랜드 레이스스터실르 피ck웰에서 79세의 나이로 사망했으며 영국 잉글랜드 노르섬프토실르 턴에 묻혔다.

ru.lifeSummary: Когда William Anthony Cave родился в 1535 г. в местности станфорд он авон, нортамптоншир, Англия, его отцу по имени Richard Cave было 29 лет, а матери, которую звали Barbara Fielding, 26. Он женился на женщине по имени Eleanor Margaret de Grey в 1560 г. в местности мелтон мавбрэи, лисестиршир, Англия. У них было по крайней мере 7 сыновей и 6 дочерей. Он умер 3 апреля 1614 г. в местности пикквелл, лисестиршир, Англия, Великобритания в возрасте 79 лет и был похоронен в местности эттон, нортамптоншир, Англия, Великобритания.
